name: tool_risk_ruleset_schema
description: Schema for LLM-generated risk rules for a single shell tool.

schema.title: Tool Risk Ruleset
schema.description: Schema for LLM-generated risk rules for a single shell tool.

tool.description: The shell tool this ruleset applies to. E.g. 'find', 'rm', 'sed'.

tool_summary.description: One sentence describing what this tool does, for use in the confirmation table.

platform.description: The platform this ruleset targets. 'posix' means the ruleset is valid for both linux and macos without platform-specific caveats. Use 'linux' or 'macos' when flag semantics differ between GNU and BSD implementations. Use 'windows' for cmd.exe/PowerShell tools with Windows-style syntax.

platform_notes.description: Optional. Describes significant behavioural differences on other platforms — e.g. 'GNU sed -i requires no argument; BSD sed -i requires a suffix argument, even if empty.' Aids the human reviewer in deciding whether a sibling ruleset is needed.

llm_confidence.description: Model's self-assessed confidence in its knowledge of this tool.

unknown_flags.description: Flags or options the model is uncertain about. Flagged for human review.

is_multiplexer.description: True if this tool dispatches to subcommands (e.g. busybox, toybox). When true, the inner subcommand must be unwrapped and assessed separately before this ruleset applies.

option_abbreviations.description: Map of known GNU long-option abbreviations to their canonical forms. Used by the argument scanner to expand abbreviated flags before pattern matching. E.g. { '--compress-prog': '--compress-program' }.

rules.description: Ordered list of rules. Evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins unless all_match is true.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this rule matches and why it is risky.

patterns.description: List of flag/argument patterns that trigger this rule.

pattern.description: The pattern string. Semgrep pattern syntax for bash.

example.description: Concrete example command that matches this pattern.

match.description: Structured evaluable form of this pattern. Used by the shard's runtime evaluator. 'pattern' is for Semgrep offline verification; 'match' is for runtime evaluation. Both must be populated.

flags_any.description: Rule fires if any of these flags are present in the parsed command.

flags_all.description: Rule fires only if ALL of these flags are present in the parsed command.

args_any.description: Rule fires if any of these values appear in the parsed arguments.

args_none.description: Rule fires only if NONE of these values appear in the parsed arguments. Use for 'flag present but argument absent' patterns.

raw_pattern.description: Optional regex applied to the raw command string. Use only when flag/arg matching is insufficient — e.g. for subshell detection or line-continuation sequences.

risk_tags.description: Intrinsic risk categories triggered by this rule.

reversible.description: Whether the effect of this command can be undone. 'depends' requires a note.

reversible_note.description: Required when reversible is 'depends'. Explains the condition.

severity.description: INFO: safe default. WARNING: notable risk. ERROR: destructive or irreversible.

all_match.description: If true, all patterns must match (AND). Default false means any match (OR).

notes.description: Optional. Flag combinations, edge cases, or caveats the reviewer should check.

likely_consequences.description: Optional. Maps this rule's risk to user-facing outcome categories (from OpenAgentSafety taxonomy). Aids non-technical users in understanding impact.

default_rule.description: Fallback rule applied when no other rule matches. Should reflect the tool's safest baseline behavior.
